trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade, significantly reduced since the Gaza conflict. China exports electronics, machinery, and consumer goods. Israel exports high-tech products, semiconductors, and agricultural technology. US pressure has substantially curtailed Israeli tech exports to China (defense-linked systems).
disputes: US pressure on Israel to restrict tech exports to China
Chinese support for Palestinian cause at UN post-October 2023

diplomatic.summary: Relations have deteriorated following October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Gaza war. China refused to condemn Hamas, backed ceasefire resolutions Israel opposed, and maintained pro-Palestinian positions. China also deepened ties with Iran, Israel's principal adversary. Prior to 2023 there was a pragmatic economic relationship despite ideological differences.
recent_incidents: China refused to condemn Hamas attacks (October 2023)
China voted for Gaza ceasefire resolutions opposed by Israel
China's deepening Iran ties alarm Israel
Previous US-Israel joint pressure on Israeli tech exports to China

military.summary: No military alliance. Israel is a close US ally; China is a strategic US competitor. US pressure ended historical Israeli-Chinese defense cooperation (HAR-PIE radar deal canceled under US pressure in 2000). No arms transfers. Israel shares US concerns about Chinese military modernization.
incidents: Historic HAR-PIE radar deal canceled under US pressure (2000)
US-Israel joint restrictions on tech exports to China

financial.summary: Chinese investment in Israeli tech startups has been curtailed by US pressure and Israeli security reviews. Some Chinese firms still operate in Israel. Israel tech companies export software and IP to China through subsidiaries. Financial links are declining.

energy.summary: Limited energy trade. Israel may export Eastern Mediterranean natural gas that reaches Asian markets. China imports Israeli agricultural tech for food security applications.

scientific.summary: Previously active scientific cooperation in computing, biotech, and water technology. Now under significant scrutiny due to US pressure and security concerns. Joint publications declining. Some Weizmann Institute and Technion partnerships continue at individual level.
